name: SBLGNT
description: Society of Biblical Literature Greek New Testament. High-quality critical text. Imposes conditions on downstream works — read the license carefully.

name: Nestle 1904
description: Eberhard Nestle's 1904 GNT. Public domain. Used as base text in several treebanks.

name: Byzantine Majority Text (Robinson-Pierpont RP2018)
description: Robinson-Pierpont Byzantine Textform 2018. Public domain. TEI-XML directly collatable with Münster INTF transcriptions via CollateX. Use RP2018 — RP2005 had accent errors.

name: Antoniades 1904/1912 Patriarchal Edition
description: Ecumenical Patriarchate edition with Robinson's morphological parsing and Strong's numbers. Narrower manuscript base than Robinson-Pierpont.

name: CNTR Statistical Restoration (SR)
description: World's first computer-generated GNT based on statistical analysis of the earliest manuscripts. Alan Bunning / Center for New Testament Restoration.

name: Westcott-Hort
description: Classic critical text, as edited by Joshua Grauman.

name: CNTR Electronic Transcriptions
description: Alan Bunning's transcriptions of every extant Greek NT manuscript to 400 AD. Collation, apparatus, and viewer at greekcntr.org.

name: Codex Sinaiticus
description: Complete digital edition of Codex Sinaiticus (4th c.) with TEI XML transcription download.

name: Amsterdam Database of NT Conjectural Emendation
description: Scholarly conjectural emendations to the Greek NT with attribution.

name: Macula LXX
description: Word-level morphology for the Septuagint, same schema as Macula Greek NT. Anchored to Macula node IDs.
notes: LXX data is in the LXX folder of the macula-greek repo.

name: CATSS LXX Morphology
description: Computer-Assisted Tools for Septuagint Studies morphological text. Not freely licensed — see user agreement before use.
acquire: See user agreement and request access from http://ccat.sas.upenn.edu/gopher/text/religion/biblical/lxxmorph/

name: Swete's Septuagint
description: Machine-corrected version. Morphological tagging in progress.

name: Macula Hebrew
description: Word-level morphology for the entire Hebrew Bible. Lemma, gloss, POS, gender/number/person, pronominal suffixes. WLC-based. Go-to for pipeline work.

name: OpenScriptures Hebrew Bible (morphhb)
description: Parsed Hebrew Bible with Strong's numbers and morphology tags. MIT licensed.

name: ETCBC / BHSA (Biblia Hebraica Stuttgartensia Amstelodamensis)
description: The most complete Hebrew syntactic database: clause, phrase, and word nodes with full feature set. Accessed via the text-fabric Python library.
acquire: In Python: from tf.app import use; A = use('etcbc/bhsa')

name: Shebanq
description: ETCBC Hebrew Bible data as a queryable SQL database with a web query interface.

name: Macula Greek NT
description: Word-level morphology anchored to SBLGNT. Same schema as Macula Hebrew. Standard for pipeline work.

name: MorphGNT (SBLGNT)
description: Per-word morphology for SBLGNT. Original open high-quality GNT morphology by James Tauber.

name: MorphGNT (Nestle1904)
description: Nestle 1904 with MorphGNT conventions.

name: MorphGNT (Tischendorf)
description: Tischendorf GNT with MorphGNT morphology.

name: Clear-Bible Speaker Quotations
description: Word-level identification of quotations in OT and NT with speaker attribution.

name: GBI Treebank / Lowfat Trees (Nestle1904 & SBLGNT)
description: Global Bible Initiative HPSG constituent grammar treebank, plus Lowfat transformation for XQuery-friendly access. Best for BaseX/XQuery work.

name: PROIEL Treebank
description: High-quality dependency trees for Tischendorf GNT. Part of a parallel corpus spanning Greek, Armenian, Gothic, Old Church Slavonic, Latin.

name: Levinsohn LGNTDF
description: Stephen Levinsohn's complete discourse feature markup of the Greek NT (UBS4/NA27). 806+ word-level annotations. Released by SIL International. 2016 Biblical Humanities Dataset of the Year.
license: Custom — freely distributable, not for sale. See https://github.com/biblicalhumanities/levinsohn/blob/master/LICENSE.md

name: Abbott-Smith
description: A Manual Greek Lexicon of the New Testament. Best-quality public domain Greek NT lexicon.

name: Liddell-Scott-Jones (LSJ)
description: A Greek-English Lexicon. The standard unabridged classical Greek lexicon. Unicode version at gcelano/LSJ_GreekUnicode.

name: Dodson Greek Lexicon
description: Simple glosses and short definitions for GNT vocabulary. Public domain.

name: Mounce Concise Dictionary
description: Mounce's Concise Greek-English Dictionary of Biblical Greek.

name: Strong's Dictionary
description: Greek and Hebrew Strong's numbers with definitions. Widely used for cross-reference.

name: UBS Semantic Dictionary of Biblical Hebrew (SDBH)
description: UBS semantic domain lexicon for the Hebrew Bible. Word-level domain annotations in Macula Hebrew.

name: UBS Semantic Dictionary of the Greek NT (SDGNT)
description: UBS semantic domain lexicon for the GNT.

name: UBS Thematic Lexicons (Flora, Fauna, Realia)
description: Three lexicons for plants, animals, and man-made objects in the Bible. Issued under open license March 2025.

name: STEPBible TIPNR
description: Translators Individualised Proper Names with all References. Tyndale House. High-quality proper noun data.
notes: TIPNR is one of several datasets in this repo.

name: Patristic Text Archive TIPNR
description: JSON/XML versions of TIPNR with persons and places separated.

name: ACAI
description: People, places, deities, groups, fauna, flora, realia, and keyterms anchored to Macula node IDs. Spans both testaments. Rick Brannan / Mission Mutual. The most comprehensive open entity annotation set.

name: OpenBible.info Bible Geocoding Data
description: Geocoded coordinates for biblical place names with passage references.

name: Theographic Bible Metadata (viz.bible)
description: Robert Rouse's structured metadata for biblical people, places, events, and relationships. Used by ACAI.

name: Pleiades
description: Gazetteer of ancient world places (Greek, Roman, Near Eastern). Coordinates, name variants, periods, references. The scholarly standard for ancient geography.

name: First1KGreek / Open Greek and Latin
description: TEI P5 XML for Josephus, Philo, Apostolic Fathers, and much of the first 1000 years of Greek literature. Essential for Hellenistic context corpora.

name: Perseus Digital Library
description: Massive collection of Greek and Latin classical texts in TEI XML with morphological tagging. Homer, Plato, Thucydides, Cicero, and more.

name: Patrologia Graeca (Migne)
description: Massive collection of Church Fathers, volumes 1–161. Open Greek and Latin project.
acquire: git clone individual volume repos under https://github.com/OGL-PatrologiaGraecaDev

name: Cramer's Catenae
description: For each NT verse, lists comments by early Church Fathers.

name: Coptic Scriptorium
description: Coptic corpus with morphology and phrasing. Essential for early Christian documentary and literary texts.
acquire: git clone individual repos at https://github.com/CopticScriptorium

name: Papyri.info / DDBDP
description: Duke Databank of Documentary Papyri. Documentary papyri (letters, contracts, receipts) in EpiDoc XML. Best source for Koine Greek in real-world contexts.

name: PHI Greek Inscriptions
description: Packard Humanities Institute corpus of Greek epigraphic texts. Requires access request.

name: EAGLE / EDH
description: Europeana network for ancient Greek and Latin epigraphic resources. EDH for Latin inscriptions.

name: Trismegistos
description: Portal for ancient papyri and inscriptions. Cross-references between collections.

name: Sefaria
description: Jewish text library: Tanakh with commentaries (Rashi, Ibn Ezra, Nachmanides), Mishnah, Talmud, Midrash, Targums, Hebrew grammar, and more.

name: Copenhagen Alliance Versification Specification
description: Canonical versification schemes (ORG, ENG, and others) for cross-translation reference alignment.

name: Clear-Bible Alignments (Biblica)
description: Word-level alignment of translations to Macula Hebrew/Greek. Used by ACAI for Markdown visualization.

name: Scripture Burrito
description: Standard for packaging and exchanging Scripture-related data. Exchange format for Paratext.

name: Paratext USFM/USX (usfmtc)
description: Access local Paratext projects programmatically via usfmtc (Martin Hosken / SIL).

name: USX 3.1 Schema
description: XML serialization of USFM 3.1 maintained by United Bible Societies.
